title: AWS Networking Quiz
description: Test your knowledge on VPCs, Subnets, NAT Gateways, and Security Groups.

questions.question: What specifically defines a subnet as 'Public' in an AWS VPC?

options.text: It has 'Public' in the subnet name tag.
It is assigned a CIDR block starting with 10.0.
Its associated Route Table contains a default route (0.0.0.0/0) pointing to an Internet Gateway.
It does not use a Security Group.

questions.question: If an EC2 instance in a Private Subnet needs to download security patches from the internet, what networking component must be configured?

options.text: A NAT Gateway living in a public subnet.

questions.question: Which AWS firewall mechanism operates at the instance level and is stateful (return traffic is automatically allowed)?
When defining a CIDR block for a subnet, how many IP addresses does AWS permanently reserve and make unavailable for your use?
How are Network ACLs fundamentally different from Security Groups?

options.text: NACLs are stateful, preventing you from writing outbound rules.
NACLs operate at the Subnet level and are stateless, requiring explicit inbound and outbound rules.
NACLs are used only for IPv6 traffic.
NACLs are the only way to allow SSH access.
